title: Master Jar & Bottle Opener, Adjustable Multifunctional Stainless Steel Can Opener Jar Lid Gripper, Manual, Kitchen Accessories
description:
About this item
1. The minimum opening of the lid is about 1.18 inches, and the maximum opening of the lid is about 5 inches. Larger lids will not open smoothly and require more force.
2. The maximum length adjustment range is 5.3 inches.
3 Product is approximately 7.7 inches long and 1.18 inches wide
4.Before buying and using it, please read the descriptions and videos on how to use it. If it is not stuck firmly, it may slip, so please watch the video or instructions carefully before using it.
reviews_5s:
Works great on certain covers....ideal for seniors who are loosing their grip strength.Outstanding, easy to use, compact and fair priced container opener.I have bad arthritis in my hands and cannot open most jars. I give them to my husband and with lots of
strength he can get them open. We looked on Amazon and decided to order this product and give it a
try. IT WORKED!!!! I can now open most jars without too much difficulty. I do recommend this product.Opens small to medium and some of the larger bottle sizes. It took me a bit to learn to use it, so I will make this an instructional review: 1. Losen the leveler and adjust to the size of the bottle or can 2. Lock the leveler. 3. Open...and viola!Works for meEverythingOpens the hardest jars!!! Love it. Bought two just to be sure we always have one.easyit did as advertisedI've looked for a lot of products under the umbrella of "arthritis" or "seniors" or "weak hands" and I've seen a lot of products with 3 or 5 holes for specific shapes of jars, with a single-hinge kind of design, and I knew that was no good for longevity.

This thing is PERFECT for my needs. It's adjustable to all kinds of lid sizes, thanks to the grooves cut on the inside of the long sides, and is completely battery-and-electricity-and-smartphone-app free! There's only two moving parts, it doesn't need dishwashing (cuz the outside of your jar should be clean when you go to open it anyway), it's completely emergency-proof!

That said, there is a bit of a learning curve. I cut horizontal lines into the sides of the plastic jar lid I used this on the first time or two! Those jaws mean business!

I learned I have to place the tool down onto the jar "upside down" so that smooth parts of the tool are on top, with the handle piece to my left. I then have to move the sliding piece into place on the opposite side of the jar lid, then squeeze down the small angled piece with my left hand, which locks the little metal tooth into the grooves on the inside of the handle, so the tool no longer adjusts its diameter around the jar lid. Finally, while squeezing, I turn the handle it towards my body (and say out loud, "righty tighty, lefty loosey"), while holding the jar steady with my right hand. That has given me the most consistent success.
reviews_1s:
This device in photographs and in general concept is convincing: finally a tool which allows a person to solidly grip a lid and twist it off. This year I purchased this and a different kind of tool and both failed miserably to do what was expected. I should have paid greater attention to the size and some of the details and maybe, just maybe, I wouldn't have wasted my money on this.

The jar grip. This is a toothed rim which may work to bite into a plastic jar lid of a certain size. I am not completely sure what it is meant to work with. All I know is that it won't grip any lid I work with. What I thought I saw was a grip made of rubber of great enough width that when compressed against a circular lid on both sides would hang on while the lid was loosened. It is not there. The grip is useless.

The lock. This is handle that looks like it locks down and keeps the grip tight while you then move the overall device anti-clockwise (or to the left). This doesn't lock anything. It engages with a toothed surface but does not stay engaged. You can't keep pushing this down to keep the grip while also attempting to rotate the entire device. Instead I was expecting more of a vice-grip sort of approach that allows the grip to be tightened and locked in place. That is what I thought I saw in the pictures. It is not so in reality.

The length. I really wanted this for lids which have a diameter of more than a few inches. They are tough to loosen. You need the length to be at least twice the expected maximum jar diameter to get the leverage needed to twist off the lid. The key element to making this dislodge difficult seals is the leverage. This device is too small to accomodate anything but the smallest lids.

In short, this device was meant to look like something that should work and fails miserably.First time out of the box to use on a large 4 inch lid jar of Pickles. I could easily have opened it by hand but wanted to try this gadget I bought for my wife who has strength issues in her hands. Gripped this to the outer edge of the lid and proceeded to try to turn. This thing CUT INTO THE LID AND PUNCTURED IT. It DID NOT turn the lid in any way shape or form. It cut the metal lid! I'm hoping it did not render the lid useless for sealing the Pickles in that brand new jar. I had to actually work the "blade" out of the metal lid. The "teeth" on the opener barely gripped and as I turned it with barely any pressure, it then slid and then cut into and through the lid. Bad design. Bad product. Don't waste your money. Buy something else. Getting so tired of cheap crap like this being sold on Amazon. Giving this 1 star because it IS metal, and it did LOOK like the pictures. And it didnt fall apart like some reviews have mentioned. But taking for stats off because IT DOES NOT FUNCTION AS ADVERTISED.This product is cheaply built and doesn’t fit standard lids Properly. The lever to ratchet it down to the lid is too short and very hard to cinch down. It also squeezes the lid at the bottom of the lid and makes it tighter on the jar.This opener does not work. There is no tension or spring to engage the holding arm. The teeth on the side rail do not hold the lid clamp, they do not even engage. either by design or workmanship this product does not work at all. I will toss this into the recycle bin. It isn't worth the trouble to return.My device on first time use did not allow the lid to be removed. It is being returned with a lid attached. LOLIts too loose, so, when applied, the handle mechanism slips out of the grooves.
I thought of tightening the moving parts by bending them, but if it still didn't work, I wouldn't be
able to return it.
MUCH TOO FLIMSY TO WORK ON LARGE CAPS.Ask yourself who is it that needs an implement that Helps one to take off a bottle top or a lid on a jar? Most of the answers that I've got were that older people who have trouble gripping things are the ones who need this kind of tool. I will say the manufacturer of this is top shelf, it is a high-quality steel. The problem is that it is very intricate and difficult to place on a bottle top or a jar. This is coming from a man who has lost most of his dexterity and needs a tool that is easy to use to get these things done. Don't buy this if you are an old person and you need help taking off tops.I was excited to get this. The reviews were good and it mentioned it being good for people with arthritis. I found it hard to use and just as difficult as opening a jar without it. When I got it to latch correctly it ruined the jar lid and I wasn’t able to use the lid again. Maybe I got a bad one? It seems on the cheap side and was just not comfortable for me.Very hard to get jar cap engaged with opener. Threw it away.Does not grip good and slips off, threw it away.
